heading: Health Information Technology Advisory Committee
label: 300jj–12 Health Information Technology Advisory Committee

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: There is established a Health Information Technology Advisory Committee (referred to in this section as the “HIT Advisory Committee”) to recommend to the National Coordinator, consistent with the implementation of the strategic plan described in , policies, and, for purposes of adoption under , standards, implementation specifications, and certification criteria, relating to the implementation of a health information technology infrastructure, nationally and locally, that advances the electronic access, exchange, and use of health information. Such Committee shall serve to unify the roles of, and replace, the HIT Policy Committee and the HIT Standards Committee, as in existence before .section 300jj–11(c)(3) of this titlesection 300jj–14 of this titleDecember 13, 2016

children.heading: Recommendations on policy framework to advance an interoperable health information technology infrastructure
children.label: 1 Recommendations on policy framework to advance an interoperable health information technology infrastructure

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The HIT Advisory Committee shall recommend to the National Coordinator a policy framework for adoption by the Secretary consistent with the strategic plan under for advancing the target areas described in this subsection. Such policy framework shall seek to prioritize achieving advancements in the target areas specified in subparagraph (B) of paragraph (2) and may, to the extent consistent with this section, incorporate policy recommendations made by the HIT Policy Committee, as in existence before .section 300jj–11(c)(3) of this titleDecember 13, 2016

children.heading: Updates
children.label: B Updates
children.text: The HIT Advisory Committee shall propose updates to such recommendations to the policy framework and make new recommendations, as appropriate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The HIT Advisory Committee shall recommend to the National Coordinator for purposes of adoption under , standards, implementation specifications, and certification criteria and an order of priority for the development, harmonization, and recognition of such standards, specifications, and certification criteria. Such recommendations shall include recommended standards, architectures, and software schemes for access to electronic individually identifiable health information across disparate systems including user vetting, authentication, privilege management, and access control.section 300jj–14 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: Achieving a health information technology infrastructure, nationally and locally, that allows for the electronic access, exchange, and use of health information, including through technology that provides accurate patient information for the correct patient, including exchanging such information, and avoids the duplication of patient records.

children.label: ii
children.text: The promotion and protection of privacy and security of health information in health information technology, including technologies that allow for an accounting of disclosures and protections against disclosures of individually identifiable health information made by a covered entity for purposes of treatment, payment, and health care operations (as such terms are defined for purposes of the regulation promulgated under section 264(c) of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996), including for the segmentation and protection from disclosure of specific and sensitive individually identifiable health information with the goal of minimizing the reluctance of patients to seek care.

children.label: iii
children.text: The facilitation of secure access by an individual to such individual’s protected health information and access to such information by a family member, caregiver, or guardian acting on behalf of a patient, including due to age-related and other disability, cognitive impairment, or dementia.

children.label: iv
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (D), any other target area that the HIT Advisory Committee identifies as an appropriate target area to be considered under this subparagraph.

children.label: i
children.text: The use of health information technology to improve the quality of health care, such as by promoting the coordination of health care and improving continuity of health care among health care providers, reducing medical errors, improving population health, reducing chronic disease, and advancing research and education.

children.label: ii
children.text: The use of technologies that address the needs of children and other vulnerable populations.

children.label: iii
children.text: The use of electronic systems to ensure the comprehensive collection of patient demographic data, including at a minimum, race, ethnicity, primary language, and gender information.

children.label: iv
children.text: The use of self-service, telemedicine, home health care, and remote monitoring technologies.

children.label: v
children.text: The use of technologies that meet the needs of diverse populations.

children.label: I
children.text: data for use in quality and public reporting programs;

children.label: II
children.text: public health; or

children.label: III
children.text: drug safety.

children.label: vii
children.text: The use of technologies that allow individually identifiable health information to be rendered unusable, unreadable, or indecipherable to unauthorized individuals when such information is transmitted in a health information network or transported outside of the secure facilities or systems where the disclosing covered entity is responsible for security conditions.

children.label: viii
children.text: The use of a certified health information technology for each individual in the United States.

children.heading: Authority for temporary additional priority target areas
children.label: D Authority for temporary additional priority target areas
i
children.text: the area is so identified for purposes of responding to new circumstances that have arisen in the health information technology community that affect the interoperability, privacy, or security of health information, or affect patient safety; and

children.label: ii
children.text: at least 30 days prior to treating such area as if it were a target area described in subparagraph (B), the National Coordinator provides adequate notice to Congress of the intent to treat such area as so described.

children.heading: Focus of committee work
children.label: E Focus of committee work
children.text: It is the sense of Congress that the HIT Advisory Committee shall focus its work on the priority areas described in subparagraph (B) before proceeding to other work under subparagraph (C).

children.heading: Rules relating to recommendations for standards, implementation specifications, and certification criteria
children.label: 3 Rules relating to recommendations for standards, implementation specifications, and certification criteria

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The HIT Advisory Committee shall recommend to the National Coordinator standards, implementation specifications, and certification criteria described in subsection (a), which may include standards, implementation specifications, and certification criteria that have been developed, harmonized, or recognized by the HIT Advisory Committee or predecessor committee. The HIT Advisory Committee shall update such recommendations and make new recommendations as appropriate, including in response to a notification sent under . Such recommendations shall be consistent with the latest recommendations made by the Committee.section 300jj–14(a)(2)(B) of this title

children.heading: Harmonization
children.label: B Harmonization
children.text: The HIT Advisory Committee may recognize harmonized or updated standards from an entity or entities for the purpose of harmonizing or updating standards and implementation specifications in order to achieve uniform and consistent implementation of the standards and implementation specification.

children.heading: Pilot testing of standards and implementation specifications
children.label: C Pilot testing of standards and implementation specifications
children.text: In the development, harmonization, or recognition of standards and implementation specifications, the HIT Advisory Committee for purposes of recommendations under paragraph (2)(B), shall, as appropriate, provide for the testing of such standards and specifications by the National Institute for Standards and Technology under .section 17911(a) of this title

children.heading: Consistency
children.label: D Consistency
children.text: The standards, implementation specifications, and certification criteria recommended under paragraph (2)(B) shall be consistent with the standards for information transactions and data elements adopted pursuant to .section 1320d–2 of this title

children.heading: Special rule related to interoperability
children.label: E Special rule related to interoperability
children.text: Any recommendation made by the HIT Advisory Committee after , with respect to interoperability of health information technology shall be consistent with interoperability as described in .December 13, 2016section 300jj of this title

children.heading: Forum
children.label: 4 Forum
children.text: The HIT Advisory Committee shall serve as a forum for the participation of a broad range of stakeholders with specific expertise in policies, including technical expertise, relating to the matters described in paragraphs (1), (2), and (3) to provide input on the development, harmonization, and recognition of standards, implementation specifications, and certification criteria necessary for the development and adoption of health information technology infrastructure nationally and locally that allows for the electronic access, exchange, and use of health information.

children.heading: Schedule
children.label: 5 Schedule
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date on which the HIT Advisory Committee first meets, such HIT Advisory Committee shall develop a schedule for the assessment of policy recommendations developed under paragraph (1). The HIT Advisory Committee shall update such schedule annually. The Secretary shall publish such schedule in the Federal Register.

children.heading: Public input
children.label: 6 Public input
children.text: The HIT Advisory Committee shall conduct open public meetings and develop a process to allow for public comment on the schedule described in paragraph (5) and recommendations described in this subsection. Under such process comments shall be submitted in a timely manner after the date of publication of a recommendation under this subsection.

children.heading: Measured progress in advancing priority areas
children.label: c Measured progress in advancing priority areas

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of this section, the National Coordinator, in collaboration with the Secretary, shall establish, and update as appropriate, objectives and benchmarks for advancing and measuring the advancement of the priority target areas described in subsection (b)(2)(B).

children.heading: Annual progress reports on advancing interoperability
children.label: 2 Annual progress reports on advancing interoperability
i
children.text: achieving a health information technology infrastructure, nationally and locally, that allows for the electronic access, exchange, and use of health information; and

children.label: ii
children.text: meeting the objectives and benchmarks described in paragraph (1).

children.label: i
children.text: a description of the work conducted by the HIT Advisory Committee during the preceding fiscal year with respect to the areas described in subsection (b)(2)(B);

children.label: ii
children.text: an assessment of the status of the infrastructure described in subparagraph (A), including the extent to which electronic health information is appropriately and readily available to enhance the access, exchange, and the use of electronic health information between users and across technology offered by different developers;

children.label: iii
children.text: the extent to which advancements have been achieved with respect to areas described in subsection (b)(2)(B);

children.label: I
children.text: achieving the objectives and benchmarks established under paragraph (1); and

children.label: II
children.text: furthering interoperability throughout the health information technology infrastructure;

children.label: v
children.text: recommendations for addressing the gaps identified in clause (iii); and

children.label: vi
children.text: a description of additional initiatives as the HIT Advisory Committee and National Coordinator determine appropriate.

children.heading: Significant advancement determination
children.label: 3 Significant advancement determination
children.text: The Secretary shall periodically, based on the reports submitted under this subsection, review the target areas described in subsection (b)(2)(B), and, based on the objectives and benchmarks established under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall determine if significant advancement has been achieved with respect to such an area. Such determination shall be taken into consideration by the HIT Advisory Committee when determining to what extent the Committee makes recommendations for an area other than an area described in subsection (b)(2)(B).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The National Coordinator shall take a leading position in the establishment and operations of the HIT Advisory Committee.

children.label: i
children.text: no fewer than 2 members are advocates for patients or consumers of health information technology;

children.label: ii
children.text: 3 members are appointed by the Secretary, 1 of whom shall be appointed to represent the Department of Health and Human Services and 1 of whom shall be a public health official;

children.label: iii
children.text: 2 members are appointed by the majority leader of the Senate;

children.label: iv
children.text: 2 members are appointed by the minority leader of the Senate;

children.label: v
children.text: 2 members are appointed by the Speaker of the House of Representatives;

children.label: vi
children.text: 2 members are appointed by the minority leader of the House of Representatives; and

children.label: vii
children.text: such other members are appointed by the Comptroller General of the United States; and

children.label: B
children.text: at least reflect providers, ancillary health care workers, consumers, purchasers, health plans, health information technology developers, researchers, patients, relevant Federal agencies, and individuals with technical expertise on health care quality, system functions, privacy, security, and on the electronic exchange and use of health information, including the use standards for such activity.

children.heading: Participation
children.label: 3 Participation
children.text: The members of the HIT Advisory Committee shall represent a balance among various sectors of the health care system so that no single sector unduly influences the recommendations of the Committee.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The terms of the members of the HIT Advisory Committee shall be for 3 years, except that the Secretary shall designate staggered terms of the members first appointed.

children.heading: Vacancies
children.label: B Vacancies
children.text: Any member appointed to fill a vacancy in the membership of the HIT Advisory Committee that occurs prior to the expiration of the term for which the member’s predecessor was appointed shall be appointed only for the remainder of that term. A member may serve after the expiration of that member’s term until a successor has been appointed. A vacancy in the HIT Advisory Committee shall be filled in the manner in which the original appointment was made.

children.heading: Limits
children.label: C Limits
children.text: Members of the HIT Advisory Committee shall be limited to two 3-year terms, for a total of not to exceed 6 years of service on the Committee.

children.heading: Outside involvement
children.label: 5 Outside involvement
children.text: The HIT Advisory Committee shall ensure an opportunity for the participation in activities of the Committee of outside advisors, including individuals with expertise in the development of policies and standards for the electronic exchange and use of health information, including in the areas of health information privacy and security.

children.heading: Quorum
children.label: 6 Quorum
children.text: A majority of the members of the HIT Advisory Committee shall constitute a quorum for purposes of voting, but a lesser number of members may meet and hold hearings.

children.heading: Consideration
children.label: 7 Consideration
children.text: The National Coordinator shall ensure that the relevant and available recommendations and comments from the National Committee on Vital and Health Statistics are considered in the development of policies.

children.heading: Assistance
children.label: 8 Assistance
children.text: For the purposes of carrying out this section, the Secretary may provide or ensure that financial assistance is provided by the HIT Advisory Committee to defray in whole or in part any membership fees or dues charged by such Committee to those consumer advocacy groups and not-for-profit entities that work in the public interest as a party of their mission.

children.heading: Application of chapter 10 of title 5
children.label: e Application of chapter 10 of title 5
children.text: Chapter 10 of title 5, other than , shall apply to the HIT Advisory Committee.section 1013 of title 5

children.heading: Publication
children.label: f Publication
children.text: The Secretary shall provide for publication in the Federal Register and the posting on the Internet website of the Office of the National Coordinator for Health Information Technology of all policy recommendations made by the HIT Advisory Committee under this section.
